name: Geoparque de Granada – circular desde Galera por Orce
region_intro: Mountain bike trails Galera are set within the Geoparque de Granada, offering diverse and challenging landscapes. The region features a mix of gullies, badlands, and arid terrain, providing varied riding experiences. Mountainous areas and vast southern deserts characterize the vistas, with geological formations creating a unique backdrop for cycling. This combination ensures dynamic and visually engaging routes for mountain bikers. Best mountain bike trails around Galera The most popular mountain bike trail is Geoparque de Granada – circular desde Galera por Orce, a 25.0 miles (40.3 km) moderate trail that takes 3 hours 31 minutes to complete. This route offers a scenic circular journey through the Geopark. Another top favourite among local mountain bikers is Orce al Bosque — circular por el Cordel de Puerto de Chirivel, a difficult 38.2 miles (61.4 km) path. This trail navigates through varied terrain, including sections of the Cordel de Puerto de Chirivel. Local mountain bikers also love the Huéscar to Mirador de la Sagra - circular via Cerro Perico Ruiz, a 46.7 miles (75.2 km) trail leading through mountainous regions and offering views towards Mirador de la Sagra, often completed in about 5 hours 27 minutes. Mountain biking around Galera is defined by arid landscapes, badlands, and mountainous regions within the Geoparque de Granada. The extensive network offers options for easy, moderate, and difficult ability levels. The routes in Galera are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 60 reviews. More than 200 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore Galera's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many mountain bike trails are there around Galera?
A: Galera offers a wide selection of mountain bike trails, with over 50 routes available. These trails cater to various skill levels, including easy, moderate, and difficult options, ensuring a suitable ride for every mountain biker.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on mountain bike trails in Galera?
A: The mountain bike trails in Galera, located within the Geoparque de Granada, feature a diverse and challenging landscape. Riders can expect a mix of gullies, badlands, technical sections, and fast tracks across spectacular arid terrain. The region also boasts mountainous areas and vast southern deserts, providing dynamic and visually stunning riding experiences.

Q: What is the best time of year for mountain biking in Galera?
A: Southern Spain generally enjoys a dry and sunny climate, making mountain biking feasible almost all year round. While summers can be hot, the cooler months are ideal for riding. Lower routes are often utilized during hotter periods, while higher tracks are preferred in cooler seasons, allowing for year-round enjoyment.

Q: Are there any circular mountain bike routes in Galera?
A: Yes, Galera offers several circular mountain bike routes. A popular moderate option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1031020332/geoparque-de-granada-circular-desde-galera-por-orce" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Geoparque de Granada – circular desde Galera por Orce&lt;/a&gt;, which takes you through the Geopark. For a more challenging circular ride, consider &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1209016067/orce-al-bosque-circular-por-el-cordel-de-puerto-de-chirivel" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Orce al Bosque — circular por el Cordel de Puerto de Chirivel&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What attractions or points of interest can I see along the mountain bike trails in Galera?
A: While exploring the mountain bike trails around Galera, you can encounter several interesting points. The town of &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5899483" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Galera&lt;/a&gt; itself is a highlight, and you might pass by historical sites such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5430451" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alcazaba of the Seven Towers&lt;/a&gt;. The routes also offer views towards other settlements like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5311929" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Huéscar&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5430435" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Parish of Our Lady of the Annunciation&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there challenging mountain bike trails for experienced riders in Galera?
A: Absolutely. Galera provides several difficult trails for experienced mountain bikers. One such route is &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1199529164/huescar-to-mirador-de-la-sagra-circular-via-cerro-perico-ruiz" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Huéscar to Mirador de la Sagra - circular via Cerro Perico Ruiz&lt;/a&gt;, which is 46.7 miles long with significant elevation gain. Another demanding option is &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1209016067/orce-al-bosque-circular-por-el-cordel-de-puerto-de-chirivel" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Orce al Bosque — circular por el Cordel de Puerto de Chirivel&lt;/a&gt;, navigating varied and technical terrain.

Q: What do other mountain bikers say about the trails in Galera?
A: The mountain bike trails around Galera are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 60 reviews. Riders often praise the diverse terrain, from gullies and badlands to mountainous regions, and the unique arid landscapes within the Geoparque de Granada.

Q: Are there any family-friendly mountain bike trails in Galera?
A: While many trails in Galera offer moderate to difficult challenges, there are easier options available. The region's extensive network includes routes suitable for less experienced riders or families looking for a gentler experience. It's always recommended to check the difficulty grade of a specific route before heading out.

Q: Can I bring my dog on the mountain bike trails in Galera?
A: The general rules for bringing dogs on trails in the Geoparque de Granada typically require dogs to be kept on a leash, especially in protected areas or near livestock. It's advisable to check local signage or specific regulations for each trail to ensure a pleasant and respectful experience for everyone.

Q: Is there parking available at trailheads in Galera?
A: While specific designated parking areas for every trailhead might vary, Galera and surrounding towns like Orce and Huéscar generally offer public parking options. It's often possible to find parking in or near the villages from where many routes start, allowing convenient access to the trails.

Q: Do I need a permit for mountain biking in the Granada Geopark?
A: Generally, mountain biking on established trails within the Geoparque de Granada does not require a specific permit for recreational use. However, it's always good practice to respect local regulations, stay on marked trails, and be mindful of the natural environment. For any specific events or large groups, it's advisable to consult local tourist information.

Q: Where can cyclists find cafes or accommodation in Galera?
A: Galera and its neighboring towns offer various options for cyclists. You'll find local cafes and restaurants to refuel after your rides. For accommodation, the area is known for its unique cave houses, which provide a distinctive cultural experience alongside more traditional lodging options.

Q: Are there any trails that connect multiple towns in the region?
A: Yes, some routes offer the opportunity to cycle between several towns, providing a broader exploration of the region. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1206217419/granada-geopark-castillejar-huescar-and-galera-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Granada Geopark: Castilléjar, Huéscar, and Galera Loop&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 42.3-mile trail that connects these three settlements, showcasing the diverse landscapes of the Geopark.

In the film La Intemperie, a place where the ruin and the well where the child was hiding were recreated.

This citadel, also known as Orce castle, is an old fortress dating from the Nasrid period. Thanks to the various reforms that have been carried out, it still preserves the seven original towers. It was declared a site of cultural interest in 1985.
